name: Oilfield Services Compute Program (US-90 corridor, tier ladder, ADC fleet model)
source_documents: colo/oilfield-services/ADC-OFS-001_Rev0_1.md (Rev 0.1, 2026-06-12, program spec - issued pending Scott approval; decision 0010)
MISSION.md (Business Line 2, play #2 - AI Enterprise for oilfield services)
telemetry_domain: Fleet surface of Telemetry: every yard node reports to Mission Control Node 0 (Marlie Hub); per-site TEL-PROFILE; ADC operates and brokers spare capacity across the fleet

parameters.value: Generic until prospects named: oilfield service companies on the US-90 corridor (Lafayette - New Iberia - Morgan City) with large yards, on-prem natural gas, HV service; yards satisfy the brownfield qualification checklist

Tier 0 edge (utility AC, ADC-NSTAR-001 SS5 sub-block) -> Tier 1 reference cluster (8->16 racks, SCALE-1) -> Tier 2 N-star block (5 MW IT + 10 MW MGN-BOD-002 on their gas) -> Tier n multi-block. Fungible up/down; every tier references its governing doc

ADC fleet model by ruling: all yard nodes report to Mission Control Node 0 (Marlie Hub); ADC operates via Telemetry and brokers/allocates spare GPU capacity across the fleet; owner gets tools + knowledge + revenue share; owner edge workloads take priority on their own iron. The aggregation answer to single-yard utilization risk.

No rental returns/prices asserted (modeled per prospect); no GPU supply commitment (NVIDIA thread separate); no revenue-share percentages until OFS-OI-04; Ornn overlay = candidate only (overlay not offtake)
Same companies are ADC-CAP-001 SS5 Plan C strategic investors - customer education doubles as investor cultivation
